mapper.select: select t1.DATA_1 , t1.LAST_UPDATE_IP , t1.CREATED_DEPT , t1.CREATION_DATE , t1.DATA_2 , t1.CREATED_BY , t1.VERSION , t1.ID , t1.LAST_UPDATE_DATE , t1.LAST_UPDATED_BY , t1.ORG_IDENTITY from DYN_TEST t1 where t1.id = #{id}
mapper.insert:
insert into DYN_TEST
DATA_1,
LAST_UPDATE_IP,
CREATED_DEPT,
CREATION_DATE,
DATA_2,
CREATED_BY,
VERSION,
ID,
LAST_UPDATE_DATE,
LAST_UPDATED_BY,
ORG_IDENTITY,
#{DATA_1,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
#{lastUpdateIp,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
#{createdDept,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
#{creationDate,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP},
#{DATA_2,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
#{createdBy,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
#{version,jdbcType=DECIMAL},
#{ID,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
#{lastUpdateDate,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP},
#{lastUpdatedBy,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
#{ORG_IDENTITY,jdbcType=VARCHAR},

insert into DYN_TEST
DATA_1,
LAST_UPDATE_IP,
CREATED_DEPT,
CREATION_DATE,
DATA_2,
CREATED_BY,
VERSION,
ID,
LAST_UPDATE_DATE,
LAST_UPDATED_BY,
ORG_IDENTITY,
select #{item.DATA_1,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , #{item.LAST_UPDATE_IP,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , #{item.CREATED_DEPT,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , #{item.CREATION_DATE,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP} , #{item.DATA_2,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , #{item.CREATED_BY,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , #{item.VERSION,jdbcType=DECIMAL} , #{item.ID,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , #{item.LAST_UPDATE_DATE,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP} , #{item.LAST_UPDATED_BY,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , #{item.ORG_IDENTITY,jdbcType=VARCHAR} from dual
mapper.update:
update DYN_TEST t1
t1.DATA_1 =#{DATA_1,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
t1.LAST_UPDATE_IP =#{lastUpdateIp,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
t1.CREATED_DEPT =#{createdDept,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
t1.CREATION_DATE =#{creationDate,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP},
t1.DATA_2 =#{DATA_2,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
t1.CREATED_BY =#{createdBy,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
t1.version =t1.version+1,
t1.ID =#{ID,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
t1.LAST_UPDATE_DATE =#{lastUpdateDate,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP},
t1.LAST_UPDATED_BY =#{lastUpdatedBy,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
t1.ORG_IDENTITY =#{ORG_IDENTITY,jdbcType=VARCHAR},
where t1.id = #{ID} and t1.version =#{version}

update DYN_TEST t1 set t1.DATA_1 =#{DATA_1,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.LAST_UPDATE_IP =#{lastUpdateIp,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.CREATED_DEPT =#{createdDept,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.CREATION_DATE =#{creationDate,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP} , t1.DATA_2 =#{DATA_2,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.CREATED_BY =#{createdBy,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.version =t1.version+1 , t1.ID =#{ID,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.LAST_UPDATE_DATE =#{lastUpdateDate,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP} , t1.LAST_UPDATED_BY =#{lastUpdatedBy,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.ORG_IDENTITY =#{ORG_IDENTITY,jdbcType=VARCHAR} where t1.id = #{ID} and t1.version =#{version}

update.foreach: update DYN_TEST t1 set t1.DATA_1 =#{DATA_1,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.LAST_UPDATE_IP =#{LAST_UPDATE_IP,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.CREATED_DEPT =#{CREATED_DEPT,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.CREATION_DATE =#{CREATION_DATE,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP} , t1.DATA_2 =#{DATA_2,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.CREATED_BY =#{CREATED_BY,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.VERSION =#{VERSION,jdbcType=DECIMAL} , t1.ID =#{ID,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.LAST_UPDATE_DATE =#{LAST_UPDATE_DATE,jdbcType=TIMESTAMP} , t1.LAST_UPDATED_BY =#{LAST_UPDATED_BY,jdbcType=VARCHAR} , t1.ORG_IDENTITY =#{ORG_IDENTITY,jdbcType=VARCHAR} where t1.id = #{item.ID} and t1.version =#{item.version};
